query: ECSS verification standard ECSS-E-ST-10-02C testing qualification acceptance

results.title: ECSS-E-ST-10-02C 6 March 2009 Space engineering Verification ECSS Secretariat
results.description: The acceptance article shall perform as the qualified product. ... All verification tools shall be verified for their intended use. ... ECSS‐Q‐ST‐40. ... See ECSS‐Q‐ST‐20‐07 for test facilities.
results.content:
The acceptance article shall perform as the qualified product. ... All verification tools shall be verified for their intended use. ... ECSS‐Q‐ST‐40. ... See ECSS‐Q‐ST‐20‐07 for test facilities.
E.g. qualification close out status at the end of ... Review (QR). ... Guidelines are in Annex G. ... Guidelines are provided in Annex G. ... Guidelines are provided in Annex G. ... Q‐ST‐10‐09, shall be raised and processed according to ECSS‐Q‐ST‐20. ... DRD in Annex C. ... Guidelines are provided in Annex G. ... Annex Q of ECSS‐E‐ST‐10.
See also ECSS‐S‐ST‐00‐01. ... Verification plan (VP), see clause 5.2.8.1. ... Assembly, integration and test (AIT) plan, see ECSS‐E‐ST‐10‐03.
Detailed requirements for Testing are covered in the ECSS E‐ST‐10‐03.
ECSS‐E‐ST‐10‐03 “Testing”. These standards are based on the same principles,

results.title: ECSS-E-ST-10-02C Rev.1 – Verification (1 February 2018) | European Cooperation for Space Standardization
results.description: The requirements on the systems engineering process are gathered in ECSS-E-ST-10 “System Engineering”; specific aspects of the SE process are further elaborated in dedicated standards, in particular: ECSS-E-ST-10-06 “Technical Specification”, ECSS-E-ST-10-02 “Verification” (the present standard), and ECSS-E-ST-10-03 “Testing”. These standards are based on the same principles, process and documentation model. The applicability of each these standards can therefore not be considered in isolation from the others · This standard may be tailored for the specific characteristic and constraints of a space project in conformance with ECSS-S-ST-00. Attachments: Docx file: ECSS-E-ST-10-02C-Rev.1(1February2018).docx PDF file: ECSS-E-ST-10-02C-Rev.1(1February2018).pdf
results.content:
The requirements on the systems engineering process are gathered in ECSS-E-ST-10 “System Engineering”; specific aspects of the SE process are further elaborated in dedicated standards, in particular: ECSS-E-ST-10-06 “Technical Specification”, ECSS-E-ST-10-02 “Verification” (the present standard), and ECSS-E-ST-10-03 “Testing”. These standards are based on the same principles, process and documentation model. The applicability of each these standards can therefore not be considered in isolation from the others · This standard may be tailored for the specific characteristic and constraints of a space project in conformance with ECSS-S-ST-00. Attachments: Docx file: ECSS-E-ST-10-02C-Rev.1(1February2018).docx PDF file: ECSS-E-ST-10-02C-Rev.1(1February2018).pdf
For verification process for SW the following standards are considered fully sufficient for development of these items: ... Detailed requirements for Testing are covered in the ECSS E-ST-10-03.
This standard does not specifically address Validation of space products as a separate process, since product Verification is performed against requirements that also address the suitability of the product to fulfill the needs of its intended use. As such, Validation is achieved through the Verification process provided adequate requirements are placed on the product. It is recognized that testing and analysis also occur during the product development process, but they are not addressed by this standard as they are not formal requirement verification activities in the sense of the customer-supplier relationship.
This Standard establishes the requirements for the verification of a space system product.

results.title: ECSS-E-ST-10-02C Verification Standards for Space Engineering - Studocu
results.description: 5.2.4 Acceptance 5.2.4.3 General a. In the acceptance stage the verification shall demonstrate that the product is free of workmanship errors and is ready for subsequent operational use.
results.content:
5.2.4 Acceptance 5.2.4.3 General a. In the acceptance stage the verification shall demonstrate that the product is free of workmanship errors and is ready for subsequent operational use.
march 2009 space engineering verification ecss secretariat requirements standards division noordwijk, the netherlands 6 march 2009 foreword this
For the purpose of this Standard, the terms and definitions from ECSS‐ST‐ 00 ‐ 01 apply, in particular for the following terms: validation verification · 3.2 acceptance stage verification stage with the objective of demonstrating that the product is free of workmanship defects, is in accordance with the qualified design and is ready for its intended use 3.2 analysis verification method performing a theoretical or empirical evaluation using techniques agreed with the Customer NOTE The selected techniques can typically include statistics, qualitative design analysis, modelling and computer simulation.
For the purpose of this Standard, the abbreviated terms from ECSS‐S‐ST‐ 00 ‐ 01 and the following apply: Abbreviation Meaning AIT assembly, integration and test AITP assembly, integration and test plan AIV assembly, integration and verification AIVP assembly, integration and verification plan AR acceptance review ARPT analysis report CDR critical design review CRR commissioning result review CP commissioning plan DRD document requirements definition ECSS European Cooperation for Space Standardization EEE electronic electrical and electromechanical EIDP end item data package EMC electro
Delta or full qualification programme (including testing), decided on a case by case basis depending on the impact of the modification. D Newly designed and developed product. Full qualification programme. 5.2.4 Acceptance 5.2.4.3 General a. In the acceptance stage the verification shall demonstrate that the product is free of workmanship errors and is ready for subsequent operational use.

meta_url.path: › technische universität berlin  › differentialgleichungen und numerik für den maschinenbau  › ecss-e-st-10-02c verification standards for space engineering

results.title: ECSS-E-ST-10-02C – Verification (6 March 2009) | European Cooperation for Space Standardization
results.description: The requirements on the systems engineering process are gathered in ECSS-E-ST-10 “System Engineering”; specific aspects of the SE process are further elaborated in dedicated standards, in particular: ECSS-E-ST-10-06 “Technical Specification”, ECSS-E-ST-10-02 “Verification” (the present standard), and ECSS-E-ST-10-03 “Testing”. These standards are based on the same principles, process and documentation model.
results.content:
The requirements on the systems engineering process are gathered in ECSS-E-ST-10 “System Engineering”; specific aspects of the SE process are further elaborated in dedicated standards, in particular: ECSS-E-ST-10-06 “Technical Specification”, ECSS-E-ST-10-02 “Verification” (the present standard), and ECSS-E-ST-10-03 “Testing”. These standards are based on the same principles, process and documentation model.
Discipline related verification aspects are complemented in Standards specific to those disciplines. Detailed requirements for Testing are covered in the ECSS E-ST-10-03.
This standard does not specifically address Validation of space products as a separate process, since product Verification is performed against requirements that also address the suitability of the product to fulfil the needs of its intended use. As such, Validation is achieved through the Verification process provided adequate requirements are placed on the product. It is recognised that testing and analysis occur during the product development process, but they are not addressed by this standard as they are not formal requirement verification activities in the sense of the customer-supplier relationship.
This Standard is intended to apply to different products at different levels from a single equipment to the overall system (including space segment hardware and software, ground segment, launchers and transportation systems, Verification tools and GSE).

results.title: ECSS-E-ST-10-03C Rev.1 – Testing (31 May 2022) | European Cooperation for Space Standardization
results.description: If relevant, qualification tests for space segment subsystems are assumed to be covered in the relevant discipline standards. Testing of hardware below space segment equipment levels (including assembly, parts, and components), Testing of stand-alone software, NOTE For verification of flight or ground software, ECSS-E-ST-40 and ECSS-Q-ST-80 apply. Testing of two-phase heat transport equipment, NOTE For acceptance and qualification testing of two-phase heat transport equipment, ECSS-E-ST-31-02 applies.
results.content:
If relevant, qualification tests for space segment subsystems are assumed to be covered in the relevant discipline standards. Testing of hardware below space segment equipment levels (including assembly, parts, and components), Testing of stand-alone software, NOTE For verification of flight or ground software, ECSS-E-ST-40 and ECSS-Q-ST-80 apply. Testing of two-phase heat transport equipment, NOTE For acceptance and qualification testing of two-phase heat transport equipment, ECSS-E-ST-31-02 applies.
Requirements for qualification, acceptance, and protoflight testing including qualification, acceptance, and proto-fight models’ test margins and duration,
